title: PTEN Genetic Test: MedlinePlus Medical Test
text:
when cells divide to make new cells. These errors can happen at any time. However, they become more common with age. 
Being exposed to certain carcinogens (substances that increase your likelihood of getting cancer). For example, exposure to UV radiation from the sun and some artificial sources like tanning beds can lead to PTEN gene changes in your skin. 
Having certain chronic viral infections. These viruses can damage your DNA and lead to PTEN gene changes that can cause cancer. 
Inherited changes in your genes are changes that you're born with. They show up in all the cells in your body, including your egg or sperm cells. That means they can be passed from parent to child.
Inherited changes in the PTEN gene aren't common. But when they happen, they can cause several disorders that trigger the growth of benign tumors (not cancer). These tumors are called hamartomas. Cowden syndrome and Bannayan-Riley-Ruvalcaba syndrome are the two main disorders associated with inherited PTEN gene changes. They are often grouped together and called PTEN hamartoma tumor syndrome (PHTS).
Hamartomas and other symptoms of PHTS can show up in infancy or later. PHTS increases your risk for certain types of cancer, such as cancer of the breast, thyroid, and kidney. However, these changes usually don't develop until adulthood.
Other names: PTEN gene, full gene analysis; PTEN sequencing and deletion/
